0.original: Action feeds and strengthens confidence. 2 00:00:03,040 --> 00:00:06,130 Inaction, in all forms, feeds fear. 3 00:00:06,400 --> 00:00:08,529 To fight fear, act. 4 00:00:08,719 --> 00:00:12,930 To increase fear, wait, put off, postpone. 5 00:00:14,240 --> 00:00:20,370 Once I heard a young paratrooper instructor explain, the jump really isn't so bad. 6 00:00:20,560 --> 00:00:23,170 It's the waiting to jump that gets a fellow. 7 00:00:23,280 --> 00:00:28,370 On the trip to the jump site, I always try to make the time pass quickly for the men. 8 00:00:28,640 --> 00:00:34,290 It's happened more than once that a trainee thought too much about what may happen and panicked. 9 00:00:34,560 --> 00:00:39,009 If we can't get him to jump the next trip, he's through as a paratrooper. 10 00:00:39,120 --> 00:00:45,009 Instead of gaining confidence, the longer he postpones the jump, the more scared he gets. 11 00:00:45,840 --> 00:00:48,690 Waiting even makes the experts nervous. 12 00:00:48,880 --> 00:00:57,969 Time magazine reported that Edward R. Murrow, the nation's top newscaster, perspires and is on edge just before he telecasts. 13 00:00:58,080 --> 00:01:01,330 But once he's in action, fear disappears. 14 00:01:01,680 --> 00:01:05,249 Many veteran actors experience the same sensation. 15 00:01:05,520 --> 00:01:09,330 They agree that the only cure for stage fright is action. 16 00:01:09,520 --> 00:01:15,330 Getting right out there before the audience is the cure for dread, worry, fear. 17 00:01:16,080 --> 00:01:23,090 Action Cures Fear One evening, we were visiting in a friend's home when their five-year-old boy, 18 00:01:23,120 --> 00:01:26,529 who had been put to bed thirty minutes before, cried out. 19 00:01:27,040 --> 00:01:36,290 The youngster had been overstimulated by a science fiction film and was afraid the little green monsters were going to enter his room and kidnap him. 20 00:01:36,719 --> 00:01:41,090 I was intrigued by the way the boy's father relieved the lad's worry. 21 00:01:41,120 --> 00:01:46,370 He didn't say, don't worry son, nothing is going to get you, go back to sleep. 22 00:01:46,719 --> 00:01:49,249 Instead, he took positive action. 23 00:01:49,440 --> 00:01:54,769 He made quite a show for the boy by inspecting the windows to be sure they were tight. 24 00:01:55,040 --> 00:02:00,690 Then he picked up one of the boy's plastic guns and put it on a table beside his bed and said, 25 00:02:00,880 --> 00:02:04,130 Billy, here's a gun for you just in case. 26 00:02:04,479 --> 00:02:07,249 The little fellow had a look of complete relief. 27 00:02:07,520 --> 00:02:10,449 Four minutes later, he was fast asleep. 28 00:02:11,440 --> 00:02:19,570 Many physicians give neutral, harmless medication to people who insist they've got to have something to make them sleep. 29 00:02:19,920 --> 00:02:26,690 To lots of folks, the act of swallowing a pill, even though unknown to them the pill has no medication, 30 00:02:26,880 --> 00:02:28,370 makes them feel better. 31 00:02:29,040 --> 00:02:37,570 It's perfectly natural to experience fear in one of many forms, but the usual methods of combating it simply don't work. 32 00:02:38,080 --> 00:02:45,410 I've been with many salesmen who tried to cure fear, which creeps up on even the most experienced of them at times, 33 00:02:45,600 --> 00:02:49,650 by going around the block a few times or drinking extra coffee. 34 00:02:49,760 --> 00:02:52,050 But these things don't get results. 35 00:02:52,400 --> 00:03:00,610 The way to combat that kind of fear, yes, any kind of fear, yes, any kind of fear, is action. 36 00:03:01,600 --> 00:03:03,810 Dread making a certain phone call? 37 00:03:04,000 --> 00:03:06,209 Make it, and dread disappears. 38 00:03:06,320 --> 00:03:10,050 Put it off, and it will get harder and harder to make. 39 00:03:10,479 --> 00:03:13,170 Dread going to a doctor for a checkup? 40 00:03:13,280 --> 00:03:15,570 Go, and your worry vanishes. 41 00:03:15,840 --> 00:03:21,489 Chances are nothing serious is wrong with you, and if there is, you know where you stand. 42 00:03:21,760 --> 00:03:28,850 Put off that checkup, and you feed your fear until it may grow so strong that you actually are sick. 43 00:03:29,520 --> 00:03:32,529 Dread discussing a problem with your superior? 44 00:03:32,719 --> 00:03:36,449 Discuss it and discover how those worries are conquered. 45 00:03:36,880 --> 00:03:38,370 Build confidence. 46 00:03:38,640 --> 00:03:41,249 Destroy fear through action.
0.b1: Taking action builds and strengthens your confidence.
0.ai_notes:
'feeds': in this context, it means to provide fuel or energy to something, making it grow stronger.
'inaction': the state of not doing anything; the opposite of taking action.
'put off': a phrasal verb meaning to delay or postpone an event or task.
'postpone': to arrange for something to take place at a time later than that first scheduled.
'paratrooper': a soldier who is trained to jump from an aircraft using a parachute.
'gets a fellow': an informal way of saying that something bothers, worries, or affects a person.
'jump site': the specific location where paratroopers are scheduled to jump.
'he's through': an idiom meaning he has failed or is finished with his career or task.
'Edward R. Murrow': a famous and highly respected American broadcast journalist from the mid-20th century.
'on edge': an idiom meaning to be nervous, tense, or anxious.
'telecasts': to broadcast a program by television.
'stage fright': the nervousness or fear that a person feels before performing in front of an audience.
'dread': a strong feeling of fear or anxiety about something that might happen.
'overstimulated': to be made too excited or active, often causing difficulty in relaxing or sleeping.
'science fiction': a genre of fiction dealing with imaginative content such as futuristic technology or space travel.
'intrigued': to be very interested in something because it is unusual or fascinating.
'made quite a show': an idiom meaning to perform an action in a deliberate or dramatic way to impress or reassure someone.
'just in case': a phrase meaning as a precaution or to be prepared for a possible problem.
'physicians': a formal word for doctors.
'neutral': having no strong effect; in this context, it refers to medicine that has no active ingredients.
'creeps up on': a phrasal verb meaning to approach someone slowly and often without them noticing.
'go around the block': to walk or drive in a circle around a city block; often used to describe wasting time to avoid a difficult task.
'checkup': a medical examination to see if a person is healthy.
'know where you stand': an idiom meaning to understand your current situation or status clearly.
'superior': a person who is higher in rank or position, such as a boss at work.
0.remarks:
'feeds': 在此语境下，指为某事物提供燃料或能量，使其变得更强。
'inaction': 不采取任何行动的状态；与采取行动相反。
'put off': 一个动词短语，意为推迟或延期某项活动或任务。
'postpone': 将某事安排在原定时间之后进行。
'paratrooper': 受过训练、使用降落伞从飞机上跳下的士兵。
'gets a fellow': 一种非正式说法，指某事困扰、担忧或影响某人。
'jump site': 伞兵预定跳伞的具体地点。
'he's through': 一个习语，意为他失败了，或者他的职业生涯或任务结束了。
'Edward R. Murrow': 20世纪中期一位著名且备受尊敬的美国广播新闻记者。
'on edge': 一个习语，意为紧张、焦虑或不安。
'telecasts': 通过电视广播节目。
'stage fright': 人在观众面前表演前感到的紧张或恐惧。
'dread': 对可能发生的事情感到强烈的恐惧或焦虑。
'overstimulated': 变得过于兴奋或活跃，常导致难以放松或入睡。
'science fiction': 处理想象内容（如未来科技或太空旅行）的小说类型。
'intrigued': 因为某事不同寻常或引人入胜而非常感兴趣。
'made quite a show': 一个习语，意为以刻意或戏剧性的方式表现，以打动或安抚他人。
'just in case': 一个短语，意为作为预防措施，或为可能出现的问题做好准备。
'physicians': 医生的正式用语。
'neutral': 没有强烈影响；在此语境下，指没有活性成分的药物。
'creeps up on': 一个动词短语，意为缓慢且常在对方未察觉的情况下接近某人。
'go around the block': 绕着街区走或开车；常用来形容为了逃避困难任务而浪费时间。
'checkup': 检查身体是否健康的医疗检查。
'know where you stand': 一个习语，意为清楚地了解自己当前的处境或地位。
'superior': 职位或地位更高的人，例如工作中的上司。
